companion_files.name: HANDOFF.md
companion_files.content:
# Handoff - Divergence Rebrand
## Feature
- Rebrand divergence execution (`PLAN.md`)

## Current State
- Phase 0 completed.
- Runtime/product implementation not started.
- 2026-02-18 readiness refresh completed against active `soundspan` repo root; workspace-governance path drift has been remediated and gates are passing.

## Last Known Notes (from root handoff migration on 2026-02-13)
1. Canonical identifiers locked:
   - front-facing: `SoundSpan`
   - internal: `soundspan`
   - org/display: `soundspan` / `soundspan.io`
   - site: `soundspan.io`
   - deep-link target: `soundspan://` with `lidify://` transition compatibility
2. Phase 0 tasks `0.1`, `0.2`, `0.3` are complete.
3. Next approved execution phase is Phase 1 (front-facing rebrand).

## Next Actions
1. Set queue top-level `task_id` and `objective` for the active rebrand execution slice.
2. Start Phase 1 Task `1.1` in `PLAN.md` after user confirmation.
3. Keep GPL attribution and legal constraints enforced during rename work.
4. Validate migration/compatibility aliases before rollout.

companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# Plan - Divergence Rebrand

## Document Status

- Last updated: 2026-02-18
- Feature: Product divergence and rebrand away from `Lidify`
- Scope: Planning/design plus staged implementation tracking for rebrand execution
- Planning maturity: `Spec Outline` complete, `Refined Spec` complete, `Detailed Implementation Plan` complete
- Implementation state: Phase 1 Tasks `1.1`-`1.3`, Phase 2 Tasks `2.1`-`2.3`, Phase 3 Tasks `3.1`-`3.4`, Phase 4 Tasks `4.1`-`4.3`, and Phase 5 Tasks `5.1`-`5.3` complete

## Planning Workflow Requirement

Before implementation begins, follow this sequence:

1. Build a spec outline.
2. Refine the spec outline.
3. Build a detailed implementation plan from the refined spec.
4. Expand each phase/task into small atomic tasks with concrete code references.
5. Obtain explicit user approval before writing implementation code.

## Purpose

Define a safe, staged path to:

1. Rebrand the fork into a distinct product name.
2. Remove front-facing `Lidify` branding first.
3. Gradually decouple internal `lidify` identifiers without breaking upgrades, user data, or compatibility.
4. Keep GPL obligations and upstream attribution compliant.

## Inputs Used

1. `AGENTS.md`
2. `.agents/plans/archived/divergence-rebrand/HANDOFF.md`
3. Existing plan docs:
   - `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-umbrella/PLAN.md`
   - `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-opensubsonic-completion/PLAN.md`
   - `.agents/plans/deferred/audiomuse-on-opensubsonic/PLAN.md` (deferred)
   - `.agents/plans/archived/mobile-native-app/PLAN.md` (historical artifact; archived/out of active scope)
4. Current branding inventory from repository grep across frontend/backend/docs/compose/charts.
5. Licensing/legal references:
   - GPLv3 text: https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html
   - GNU GPL FAQ: https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-faq.html
   - GNU free software definition notes on modified versions/name/logo: https://www.gnu.org/philosophy/free-sw.ar.html
   - FSF article (GPLv3 section 7(e), trademarks): https://www.fsf.org/blogs/licensing/more-about-the-app-store-gpl-enforcement
   - USPTO trademark confusion and filing basics: https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/likelihood-confusion
   - USPTO trademark process overview: https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/basics

## High-Level Legal and License Ramifications (Not Legal Advice)

1. GPL continuity is mandatory for distributed derivative versions:
   - Keep GPL licensing terms and notices.
   - Keep copyright notices and mark modified files/versions.
   - Provide corresponding source when conveying binaries/object code.
2. Private/self-hosted use vs distribution matters:
   - Internal/private modifications are generally unrestricted.
   - Distribution (Docker images, release tarballs, hosted client bundles sent to users) triggers GPL conveyance obligations.
   - Network-only use without conveying copies is different; AGPL-style network copyleft is not automatic under GPLv3.
3. Trademark rights are separate from copyright:
   - GPL does not automatically grant trademark rights.
   - A fork should avoid confusingly similar branding and should use a clearly distinct name/logo.
4. Attribution and anti-confusion are both required:
   - Preserve legal notices and “forked from” attribution.
   - Do not present the fork as the upstream project.
5. Practical legal workstream for divergence:
   - Name clearance (knockout trademark search + ecosystem collisions).
   - Distinct visual identity (name/logo/domain/package prefixes).
   - Project-level trademark/brand usage policy for your new name.
   - Counsel review before public launch if trademark risk tolerance is low.

## Non-Goals

1. One-shot rename of every internal token in a single PR.
2. Rewriting historical release notes to erase upstream history.
3. Replacing GPL with a non-copyleft license.
4. Providing legal advice as a substitute for counsel.

---

## Spec Outline (v1)

### Capability Slices

1. Legal/compliance foundation for independent branding.
2. Front-facing product rebrand (UI text, metadata, docs, setup flows).
3. Compatibility layer for old identifiers (storage keys, deep links, webhook names, tags).
4. Gradual internal rename of package/infrastructure identifiers.
5. Distribution and ecosystem rename (Docker/Helm/GitHub/docs domains).
6. Verification and migration testing for existing installs.

### High-Level Exit Criteria

1. New brand appears everywhere user-facing.
2. Existing users can upgrade without data loss or broken sessions/links.
3. Internal `lidify` identifiers are either removed or tracked with explicit deprecation policy.
4. Licensing and attribution remain compliant and auditable.

---

## Refined Spec (v2)

## Naming Model

1. Define canonical new identifiers:
   - `brandName` (display, title text)
   - `brandSlug` (lowercase identifier)
   - `brandScheme` (deep link URI scheme)
   - `brandUaToken` (User-Agent token)
2. Define compatibility aliases (temporary):
   - legacy URI scheme `lidify://`
   - legacy storage prefixes `lidify_`
   - legacy webhook/tag values (`Lidify`, `lidify-discovery`)

Decision snapshot (2026-02-13):

1. `brandName`: `SoundSpan` (front-facing documentation/UI title case).
2. `brandSlug`: `soundspan` (non-front-facing references use lowercase).
3. `brandOrg`: GitHub org `soundspan` (display name `soundspan.io`).
4. `brandSite`: `soundspan.io`.
5. `brandScheme`: `soundspan://` (with legacy `lidify://` compatibility in transition).
6. `brandUaToken`: `soundspan` (lowercase).

## Rebrand Scope: Front-Facing First

Primary user-facing touchpoints:

1. Next metadata and auth/setup surfaces:
   - `frontend/app/layout.tsx`
   - `frontend/app/login/page.tsx`
   - `frontend/app/onboarding/page.tsx`
   - `frontend/app/setup/page.tsx`
   - `frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`
   - `frontend/components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx`
   - `frontend/components/layout/TVLayout.tsx`
   - `frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`
   - `frontend/components/PWAInstallPrompt.tsx`
2. PWA identity:
   - `frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest`
   - `frontend/public/sw.js`
   - `frontend/scripts/generate-pwa-icons.js`
3. Backend user-visible labels:
   - `backend/src/config/swagger.ts`
   - `backend/src/index.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/auth.ts` (TOTP issuer/name)
4. Public docs and onboarding:
   - `README.md`
   - `CHANGELOG.md` (forward entries only; keep historical context intact)
   - `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`
   - `charts/lidify/README.md` (until chart rename phase)

## Gradual Internal De-`lidify` Strategy

Internal/compatibility touchpoints to phase over time:

1. Storage keys and browser state:
   - `frontend/lib/audio-state-context.tsx`
   - `frontend/lib/audio-playback-context.tsx`
   - `frontend/lib/howler-engine.ts`
   - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
   - `frontend/hooks/useActivityPanel.ts`
   - `frontend/lib/listen-together-session.ts`
2. Deep-link and protocol identifiers:
   - `frontend/app/device/page.tsx` (`lidify://link`)
3. Backend integration constants:
   - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts` (`lidify-discovery`)
   - `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts` (webhook name matching)
   - `backend/src/routes/webhooks.ts` (`service: "lidify"`)
4. External request fingerprints (UA strings):
   - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/wikidata.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/fanart.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/lyrics.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/podcastDownload.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
5. Runtime packaging/infrastructure:
   - `backend/package.json`, `frontend/package.json`
   - `docker-compose*.yml`, `.env.example`
   - `charts/lidify/*` (chart name/helper namespace/repo references)

## Compatibility Policy (Critical)

1. Read-old/write-new for storage keys during transition windows.
2. Accept old and new deep-link schemes; emit new scheme in UI.
3. Match both old/new webhook names and tag labels.
4. Keep old Docker image tags and Helm install compatibility aliases for at least one full minor release.
5. Publish deprecation timelines before removing legacy aliases.

## Legal/Compliance Deliverables

1. Add/maintain explicit attribution in `README.md` and docs:
   - “Forked from `Chevron7Locked/lidify`.”
2. Maintain GPL license text and contributor notices.
3. Add a project brand policy (`docs/BRAND_POLICY.md`) for your new mark/logo usage.
4. Capture trademark clearance decision in docs/handoff.

---

## Detailed Implementation Plan (Atomic Tasks)

### Status Legend

- `Pending`: Not started
- `In Progress`: Actively being worked
- `Complete`: Finished and includes completion summary

Completion summary template (required when task is `Complete`):

- Delivered:
- Files/functions/hooks touched:
- Verification/testing:

## Phase 0: Legal and Brand Foundation

### Task 0.1: Decide target brand identifiers and aliases
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Canonical name/slug/scheme/UA token + legacy alias policy.
- References:
  - `PLAN.md` (Naming Model)
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Locked canonical branding identifiers:
      - `brandName`: `SoundSpan`
      - `brandSlug`: `soundspan`
      - `brandOrg`: `soundspan` (display name `soundspan.io`)
      - `brandSite`: `soundspan.io`
      - `brandScheme`: `soundspan://`
      - `brandUaToken`: `soundspan`
    - Locked naming policy: all non-front-facing references remain lowercase.
    - Locked transition aliases: keep `lidify://`, `lidify_`, `Lidify`, and `lidify-discovery` compatibility during migration.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `PLAN.md`
  - Verification/testing:
    - User-provided naming directive confirmed in active session.

### Task 0.2: Trademark/name clearance pass
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Documented knockout checks (USPTO + ecosystem collisions: GitHub org/repo, DockerHub/GHCR, npm package names, domain availability).
- References:
  - https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/basics
  - https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/likelihood-confusion
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Performed preliminary knockout checks on 2026-02-13 with the following outcomes:
      - GitHub org: `https://github.com/soundspan` returns `200`; org exists with login `soundspan`, display name `soundspan.io`, created `2026-02-13`.
      - Domain DNS: `soundspan.io` has Cloudflare NS records (`amy.ns.cloudflare.com`, `igor.ns.cloudflare.com`); no public `A/AAAA` records resolved at check time.
      - DockerHub namespace: `https://hub.docker.com/u/soundspan` returns `404` (`User not found`) at check time.
      - npm package checks: `soundspan`, `@soundspan/frontend`, `@soundspan/backend` returned `404 Not Found` at check time.
      - Trademark signal (preliminary only): web-indexed historical `SOUNDSPAN` marks were found as abandoned (example index: `https://trademarks.justia.com/780/44/soundspan-78044624.html`), but this is not a substitute for attorney-grade class-by-class clearance.
    - Clearance decision: proceed with `SoundSpan` as working brand, with requirement to perform full legal clearance before broad public launch.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `PLAN.md`
  - Verification/testing:
    - Network checks via `curl`, `dig`, `npm view`, and GitHub API.
    - Source references captured in plan.

### Task 0.3: License/attribution policy lock
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit policy for GPL notices, fork attribution language, and modified-version notice requirements.
- References:
  - `LICENSE`
  - `README.md`
  - https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.en.html
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Locked license/compliance policy for divergence:
      - Keep `GPL-3.0` licensing and preserve license text in `LICENSE`.
      - Preserve upstream attribution in `README.md` (`This repository is a fork of Chevron7Locked/lidify`).
      - Preserve copyright notices.
      - Mark modified versions clearly and keep a changelog trail for divergence work.
      - For distributed binaries/images, provide corresponding source and GPL terms.
      - Do not add downstream restrictions that conflict with GPL rights.
    - Locked trademark policy boundary: naming and logo rights are managed separately from GPL copyright permissions.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `PLAN.md`
  - Verification/testing:
    - Verified GPL presence in `LICENSE`.
    - Verified fork attribution + GPL statements in `README.md`.

## Phase 1: Front-Facing Rebrand (Low-Risk, High-Visibility)

### Task 1.1: Add centralized brand constants in frontend/backend
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Shared brand constants to avoid string scatter and future rename churn.
- References:
  - `frontend/app/layout.tsx`
  - `backend/src/config/swagger.ts`
  - `backend/src/index.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added centralized brand constant modules in frontend and backend aligned to locked naming model (`SoundSpan` / `soundspan`).
    - Replaced scattered inline brand strings in target Task `1.1` files with shared constants.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `frontend/lib/brand.ts`
    - `frontend/app/layout.tsx`
    - `backend/src/config/brand.ts`
    - `backend/src/config/swagger.ts`
    - `backend/src/index.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/config/__tests__/swagger.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend run build`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- app/layout.tsx lib/brand.ts`

### Task 1.2: Replace visible product name/logo text in primary UI surfaces
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Updated app title, login/onboarding/setup text, nav branding, install prompt text.
- References:
  - `frontend/app/login/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/app/onboarding/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/app/setup/page.tsx`
  - `frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`
  - `frontend/components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx`
  - `frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`
  - `frontend/components/PWAInstallPrompt.tsx`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Replaced user-visible `Lidify` name strings in login/onboarding/sidebars/install prompt with centralized frontend brand constants.
    - Updated brand-bearing logo alt labels in primary navigation and setup/login/onboarding surfaces to use shared branding constants.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `frontend/lib/brand.ts`
    - `frontend/app/login/page.tsx`
    - `frontend/app/onboarding/page.tsx`
    - `frontend/app/setup/page.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/PWAInstallPrompt.tsx`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `rg -n "Lidify|lidify" frontend/app/login/page.tsx frontend/app/onboarding/page.tsx frontend/app/setup/page.tsx frontend/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx frontend/components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx frontend/components/layout/TopBar.tsx frontend/components/PWAInstallPrompt.tsx` (no matches)
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- app/login/page.tsx app/onboarding/page.tsx app/setup/page.tsx components/layout/Sidebar.tsx components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx components/layout/TopBar.tsx components/PWAInstallPrompt.tsx lib/brand.ts`

### Task 1.3: Rebrand metadata/PWA identity and API docs titles
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: New manifest/app names, service worker comments/cache version strategy, Swagger titles, TOTP issuer.
- References:
  - `frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest`
  - `frontend/public/sw.js`
  - `backend/src/config/swagger.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/auth.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Rebranded PWA manifest identity to `SoundSpan` and updated manifest description to the approved tagline.
    - Updated service worker branding header and cache namespace keys from `lidify-*` to `soundspan-*`.
    - Updated 2FA enrollment secret metadata to use dynamic backend brand constants for TOTP display name and issuer.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest`
    - `frontend/public/sw.js`
    - `backend/src/routes/auth.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `jq . frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest`
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/authRuntime.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/config/__tests__/swagger.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend run build`

## Phase 2: Compatibility Layer for Existing Installs

### Task 2.1: Implement storage-key migration helper (read old/write new)
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Non-breaking migration for `lidify_*` localStorage keys.
- References:
  - `frontend/lib/audio-state-context.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/audio-playback-context.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/howler-engine.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added a shared storage migration utility that reads `soundspan_*` keys first, falls back to `lidify_*`, and migrates legacy values forward.
    - Updated targeted audio/api modules to use `soundspan_*` keys for writes while preserving read compatibility with legacy `lidify_*` keys.
    - Applied two adjacent compatibility touchpoint fixes (`HowlerAudioElement` and audiobooks clear-state path) to prevent stale-key regressions after the key-prefix switch.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `frontend/lib/storage-migration.ts`
    - `frontend/lib/audio-state-context.tsx`
    - `frontend/lib/audio-playback-context.tsx`
    - `frontend/lib/howler-engine.ts`
    - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
    - `frontend/components/player/HowlerAudioElement.tsx`
    - `frontend/app/audiobooks/page.tsx`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `rg -n "lidify_(current_track|current_audiobook|current_podcast|playback_type|queue|current_index|is_shuffle|repeat_mode|player_mode|volume|muted|podcast_episode_queue|current_time|current_time_track_id|playback_device_id)" frontend` (only legacy fallback constant remains in `api.ts`)
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- lib/storage-migration.ts lib/audio-state-context.tsx lib/audio-playback-context.tsx lib/howler-engine.ts lib/api.ts components/player/HowlerAudioElement.tsx app/audiobooks/page.tsx` (warnings only; no errors)

### Task 2.2: Deep-link scheme migration with fallback
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: New deep-link scheme support while still accepting `lidify://`.
- References:
  - `frontend/app/device/page.tsx`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Updated device-link QR deep-link generation to emit `soundspan://` by default.
    - Added an explicit legacy-mode toggle to emit `lidify://` links for older mobile builds during transition.
    - Removed remaining hardcoded `Lidify` app copy in the device-link page and switched to shared brand constants.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `frontend/app/device/page.tsx`
    - `frontend/lib/brand.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `npm --prefix frontend run lint -- app/device/page.tsx lib/brand.ts`

### Task 2.3: Webhook/tag backward-compat matching
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: System accepts old and new webhook/tag names without requiring manual admin cleanup.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/webhooks.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Updated Lidarr discovery tag behavior to prefer `soundspan-discovery` for new creation while matching existing `lidify-discovery` tags for upgrade compatibility.
    - Updated Lidarr webhook auto-config matching to accept both `SoundSpan` and `Lidify` webhook names, plus URL alias patterns, before deciding whether to create/update.
    - Updated webhook verification metadata to expose current service identity with explicit legacy alias signaling.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/webhooks.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/discover.ts` (adjacent stale tag-label log/comment cleanup)
  - Verification/testing:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/webhooksRuntime.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/systemSettingsRuntime.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/services/__tests__/lidarrService.test.ts -t "discovery tag" --runInBand`

## Phase 3: Internal Identifier De-Lidify (Staged)

### Task 3.1: Rename package metadata and internal labels
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Updated `package.json` names/descriptions/repository URLs and startup logs.
- References:
  - `backend/package.json`
  - `frontend/package.json`
  - `backend/docker-entrypoint.sh`
  - `frontend/docker-entrypoint.sh`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Renamed backend/frontend package metadata to `soundspan-*` and updated descriptions from `Lidify` to `SoundSpan`.
    - Updated backend/frontend package repository URLs to the rebrand org/repo path (`github.com/soundspan/soundspan`).
    - Updated Docker entrypoint startup banners to `SoundSpan` labels.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/package.json`
    - `frontend/package.json`
    - `backend/docker-entrypoint.sh`
    - `frontend/docker-entrypoint.sh`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `npm --prefix backend run build`
    - `rg -n "lidify-backend|lidify-frontend|Lidify backend API server|Lidify web frontend|Starting Lidify|Lidify Backend starting" backend/package.json frontend/package.json backend/docker-entrypoint.sh frontend/docker-entrypoint.sh` (no matches)

### Task 3.2: Normalize external User-Agent branding token
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Updated UA tokens with compatibility-safe format.
- References:
  - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/wikidata.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/fanart.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/lyrics.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/podcastDownload.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added a shared backend brand User-Agent constant and normalized targeted external request paths to use `SoundSpan/<runtime-version> (https://github.com/soundspan/soundspan)`.
    - Removed hardcoded per-service Lidify UA strings from MusicBrainz, Wikidata, Fanart, image storage fetches, lyrics/LRCLIB calls, podcast downloads, and audiobook-cover proxy fetches in library routes.
    - Updated affected runtime tests to assert the new SoundSpan UA token behavior.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/config/brand.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/wikidata.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/fanart.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/lyrics.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/podcastDownload.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/__tests__/imageStorage.test.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/libraryRuntime.test.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/services/__tests__/imageStorage.test.ts --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend test -- src/routes/__tests__/libraryRuntime.test.ts -t "audiobook cover" --runInBand`
    - `npm --prefix backend run build`

### Task 3.3: Migrate environment defaults and compose naming
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: New container/volume/network naming with documented migration path from legacy names.
- References:
  - `.env.example`
  - `docker-compose.yml`
  - `docker-compose.aio.yml`
  - `docker-compose.dev.yml`
  - `docker-compose.local.yml`
  - `docker-compose.override.ha.yml`
  - `docker-compose.override.lite-mode.yml`
  - `docker-compose.services.yml`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Switched compose and environment defaults from legacy `lidify*` naming to `soundspan*` naming across split-stack, AIO, and local dev compose files.
    - Added explicit upgrade-compatibility controls in `.env.example` for preserving legacy Docker object names (network/container/volume) during in-place migrations.
    - Added `SOUNDSPAN_CALLBACK_URL` runtime support (with legacy `LIDIFY_CALLBACK_URL` fallback) and wired compose env defaults to the new variable.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `.env.example`
    - `docker-compose.yml`
    - `docker-compose.aio.yml`
    - `docker-compose.local.yml`
    - `docker-compose.override.lite-mode.yml`
    - `docker-compose.services.yml`

### Task 3.4: Helm chart rename strategy
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Plan + execution for chart path/name/helper namespace migration with upgrade notes.
- References:
  - `charts/lidify/Chart.yaml`

    - Migrated chart helper namespace usage from `lidify.*` to `soundspan.*` across templates, including helper definitions and `include` callsites.
    - Updated chart values/docs image repository examples and install/upgrade guidance to SoundSpan naming, with explicit in-place upgrade notes for existing `lidify` releases.
    - Added helper-level compatibility logic so legacy release names (`lidify`, `lidify-*`) keep stable object naming during upgrade.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:

## Phase 4: Documentation and Governance

### Task 4.1: Rebrand user-facing docs and examples
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Updated README/docs examples with new names/images/URLs while preserving fork attribution.
- References:
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`
  - `CHANGELOG.md`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Rebranded top-level README user-facing product naming to `SoundSpan`, updated release/image badge links, and updated quick-start Docker image/container/volume examples.
    - Rebranded reverse-proxy/tunnel guide naming/examples (`soundspan.example.com`, `soundspan-frontend`, `soundspan-backend`) for consistency with current product identity.
    - Updated `CHANGELOG.md` header + unreleased (`1.5.0`) product-name references to `SoundSpan`, while preserving historical entries and explicit upstream fork context.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `README.md`
    - `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`
    - `CHANGELOG.md`

- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: `docs/BRAND_POLICY.md` defining allowed logo/name usage and “not affiliated with upstream” language.
- References:

    - Linked the brand policy from README/documentation index and referenced it from the fork-delta ledger for policy discoverability.
    - Added changelog visibility for the new policy in the active unreleased section.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:

    - `README.md`

- Status: Complete

    - Added unreleased changelog visibility entry so timeline changes are release-tracked.

    - `CHANGELOG.md`
  - Verification/testing:

## Phase 5: Verification and Release Safety

### Task 5.1: Build regression checklist for upgrade from legacy-branded installs
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Manual/automated checklist proving old installs upgrade cleanly.
- References:
  - `frontend/tests/e2e/*`
  - `backend/src/__tests__/*`
  - `backend/scripts/manual-tests/*`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added and linked `docs/REBRAND_UPGRADE_CHECKLIST.md` covering policy/preflight, backend runtime checks, frontend validation, Helm compatibility checks, compose config validation, and manual upgrade gates.
    - Checklist includes explicit pass criteria and legacy-upgrade checks for deep links, storage migration, webhook/tag aliases, callback env aliasing, compose naming, and Helm release migration.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `docs/REBRAND_UPGRADE_CHECKLIST.md`
    - `docs/README.md`

### Task 5.2: Validate front-facing rebrand completeness
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Audit report showing no remaining `Lidify` references in runtime UI routes.
- References:
  - `frontend/app/*`
  - `frontend/components/*`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added `docs/REBRAND_FRONTEND_AUDIT.md` with explicit command-based evidence for frontend runtime branding validation.
    - Eliminated remaining literal `Lidify` user-facing strings and logo/favicon path references in runtime UI routes/components, and moved remaining legacy storage-key references into shared migration helpers (`frontend/lib/storage-migration.ts`) so app/component route code is clean.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `docs/REBRAND_FRONTEND_AUDIT.md`
    - `frontend/app/layout.tsx`

    - `frontend/components/layout/MobileSidebar.tsx`

    - `frontend/components/player/OverlayPlayer.tsx`
    - `frontend/lib/storage-migration.ts`
    - `frontend/public/sw.js`
    - `frontend/app/globals.css`
    - `frontend/scripts/generate-pwa-icons.js`
    - `frontend/components/ui/Button.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/ui/PlayableCard.tsx`

    - `rg -n "Lidify|LIDIFY|lidify" frontend/app frontend/components --glob '!**/*.test.*' --glob '!**/__tests__/**'`
    - `npm --prefix frontend run build`

### Task 5.3: Validate internal-identifier debt ledger
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Remaining internal `lidify` tokens tracked as `Keep/Deprecate/Remove`.
- References:
  - repository-wide grep report
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered:
    - Added `docs/LEGACY_IDENTIFIER_LEDGER.md` with explicit Keep/Deprecate/Remove classification for remaining legacy identifiers and migration rationale/targets.
    - Linked ledger in `docs/README.md` and added changelog visibility for release tracking.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `docs/LEGACY_IDENTIFIER_LEDGER.md`
    - `docs/README.md`
    - `CHANGELOG.md`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `rg -n "Lidify|LIDIFY|lidify" backend/src frontend --glob '!**/*.test.*' --glob '!**/__tests__/**'`

---

## Recommended Execution Order

1. Phase 0 (legal/name foundation)
2. Phase 1 (front-facing rebrand)
3. Phase 2 (compatibility layer)
4. Phase 3 (internal/infrastructure de-`lidify`)
5. Phase 4 (docs/governance)
6. Phase 5 (verification and release)

## Implementation Start Gate

Implementation for any phase in this document requires explicit user approval after reviewing this plan.

## Readiness Refresh (2026-02-18)

Pre-start drift audit completed before Phase 1 implementation:

1. Governance/workspace drift status:
   - Prior hard-rule mismatch to the legacy repository root was detected during audit.
   - Remediation completed: canonical workspace contract paths now target `/home/joshd/git/soundspan`.
2. Plan/queue continuity status:
   - Queue item `plan-current-divergence-rebrand` exists and remains `pending`.
   - Phase status integrity confirmed: `0.1`/`0.2`/`0.3` complete; all execution phases still pending.
3. Reference drift status in this plan:
   - Compose references were updated to match current repository files (`docker-compose.aio.yml`, `docker-compose.local.yml`, and active override files).
   - Backend test-path references were updated to current layout (`backend/src/__tests__/*`, `backend/scripts/manual-tests/*`).
4. Rebrand baseline snapshot (tracked files):
   - `SoundSpan|soundspan` occurrences: `0`.
   - `Lidify|lidify` occurrences: non-zero and expected pre-implementation baseline.
5. Phase 1 start checklist:
   - [x] `npm run policy:check` passes in `soundspan`.
   - [x] `npm run agent:preflight` passes in `soundspan`.
   - [x] `npm run index:verify` passes after preflight.
   - [x] Queue top-level `task_id` and `objective` are set for the active rebrand execution slice.
   - [x] User confirms start of Phase 1 Task `1.1`.

companion_files.name: PROMPT_HISTORY.md
companion_files.content:
# PROMPT_HISTORY.md

## 2026-02-13 Session Prompt History (Rebrand Divergence Track)
1. User instructed execution of Phase 0 in `PLAN.md`.
2. User finalized canonical branding inputs (`SoundSpan`, `soundspan`, `soundspan.io`).

## Outcome Summary
- Naming model and compatibility aliases were locked in the plan.
- Phase 0 legal/knockout/policy tasks were marked complete with verification notes.
- No runtime/product implementation code changes were made in that session.

plan.title: Plan - Divergence Rebrand
plan.objective:
Define a safe, staged path to:

1. Rebrand the fork into a distinct product name.
2. Remove front-facing `Lidify` branding first.
3. Gradually decouple internal `lidify` identifiers without breaking upgrades, user data, or compatibility.
4. Keep GPL obligations and upstream attribution compliant.
out_of_scope: 1. One-shot rename of every internal token in a single PR.
2. Rewriting historical release notes to erase upstream history.
3. Replacing GPL with a non-copyleft license.
4. Providing legal advice as a substitute for counsel.

tasks.summary:
Define a safe, staged path to:

1. Rebrand the fork into a distinct product name.
2. Remove front-facing `Lidify` branding first.
3. Gradually decouple internal `lidify` identifiers without breaking upgrades, user data, or compatibility.
4. Keep GPL obligations and upstream attribution compliant.

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals:
Define a safe, staged path to:

1. Rebrand the fork into a distinct product name.
2. Remove front-facing `Lidify` branding first.
3. Gradually decouple internal `lidify` identifiers without breaking upgrades, user data, or compatibility.
4. Keep GPL obligations and upstream attribution compliant.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals:
1. One-shot rename of every internal token in a single PR.
2. Rewriting historical release notes to erase upstream history.
3. Replacing GPL with a non-copyleft license.
4. Providing legal advice as a substitute for counsel.
